authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget / Electronic Code of Federal Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary government-wide regulation for federal award administration, including NOFO content, award requirements, reporting, procurement, records, access, remedies, opportunities to object, closeout, and audit requirements.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Lifecycle control, record validation, retention, reporting, remedies, and closeout

authoritative_sources.title: The Grant Lifecycle
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal grants portal guidance for the public grant lifecycle and role separation among grant-making agencies and applicants or recipients.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Opportunity intake, application package handling, award setup, and post-award routing
used_for: pre-award, award, and post-award stage naming

authoritative_sources.title: Grant Eligibility
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public guidance defining broad applicant eligibility categories and reinforcing that specific funding opportunities and agency rules control eligibility.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility context collection and adjudication boundary control
used_for: refusal to make official eligibility decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Grant Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal grant portal guidance describing reporting channels, FFATA/FSRS flow, progress reports, site visits, audits, and standard reporting forms.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Post-award reporting, subrecipient reporting, and audit package assembly

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Records Management and General Records Schedules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the federal authority for records schedules and disposition rules that constrain official grant record retention, transfer, and disposal.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Retention, access, disposition, and official-record governance

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO Green Book is the recognized federal internal-control standard for documenting controls, evidence, responsibilities, risk, and monitoring.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Control documentation, auditability, risk, and monitoring escalation
used_for: segregation of duties and control ownership

authoritative_sources.title: 2025 Compliance Supplement - 2 CFR Part 200 Appendix XI
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMB's Compliance Supplement is the authoritative annual audit reference for federal program compliance requirements under Single Audit rules.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audit-ready evidence packaging and compliance requirement traceability

authoritative_sources.title: HHS Grants Policy Statement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official agency grants manual used as an example of agency-specific award terms, recipient responsibilities, prior-approval boundaries, remedies, and role definitions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Agency-policy source hierarchy and tenant adaptation

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify grant request type, lifecycle phase, requested decision authority, tenant prerequisites, and adjacent-owner needs before any record action.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Opportunity and eligibility context collection
workflow_stages.description: Collect NOFO, Assistance Listing, program statute, applicant or recipient identity, UEI/SAM context, and eligibility evidence without making final eligibility or award decisions.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

workflow_stages.name: Application, award, and document validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate application forms, certifications, assurances, budget records, Notice of Award, award terms, official receipts, and system timestamps for administrative completeness.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance
Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Post-award reporting and monitoring support
workflow_stages.description: Coordinate financial and performance report evidence, FFATA/FSRS subaward reporting support, site-visit notes, subrecipient monitoring records, and due-date status.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, and public-records governance
workflow_stages.description: Check retention schedules, access permissions, protected information, public-records constraints, and official-record status before disclosure, deletion, transfer, or publication handling.
source_classes: Records, procurement, or public-service operating rules

workflow_stages.name: Exception, due-process, and adjudication escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing authority, conflicting records, remedies, opportunity-to-object/hearing/appeal facts, legal interpretation, audit opinion, and official grant decisions.
source_classes: Statutes, agency manuals, or public-program guidance

artifacts: Assistance Listing and program statute context
Notice of Award and award terms and conditions
Approved budget, cost-share, indirect-cost, and prior-approval records
Financial reports including SF-425 where applicable
Performance reports, RPPR or progress-report materials
Retention schedule, access log, correspondence log, and audit trail
systems_of_record: Case management and public-service administration systems

allowed: Classify grant administration requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate whether required records, attachments, owner facts, source context, and system links are present for administrative use.
Prepare completeness summaries, status notes, due-date trackers, evidence packets, audit-trail summaries, and escalation packages.
Recommend next administrative steps and owner routing without claiming official award, eligibility, legal, audit, public-records, or enforcement authority.
escalate: Required NOFO, program statute, agency policy, award terms, official receipts, owner facts, or tenant policies are missing.
Uniform Guidance, agency manual, NOFO, award terms, tenant policy, or system-of-record data materially conflict.
Eligibility, rights, benefits, obligations, remedies, hearings, appeals, public-records disclosure, PII, audit opinion, or procurement authority affects the requested outcome.
Record retention, access, publication, or disposition questions cannot be resolved from documented authority.
refuse: Request asks the lane to approve, deny, score, rank, terminate, suspend, or otherwise adjudicate a grant.
Request asks for legal interpretation, cost-allowability decision, enforcement posture, audit opinion, financial approval, or public-records disclosure decision.
Required records or authoritative context are missing and would change the outcome.
Stakeholder asks to alter official grant records without documented authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and auditable change trail.

domain_failure_modes: Treating administrative completeness as award approval, eligibility determination, cost-allowability decision, or legal conclusion.
Changing official grant records or status flags without delegated authority, source-of-truth confirmation, and audit trail.
Proceeding when NOFO, Assistance Listing, program statute, agency policy, Notice of Award, or tenant source hierarchy is missing or conflicting.
Publishing, deleting, or withholding grant records without resolving retention, protected information, public-records, or FOIA constraints.
Suppressing conflicting system records instead of preserving both states and escalating to the official owner.
Failing to preserve notice, objection, hearing, appeal, remedy, or termination evidence where rights or obligations are affected.
Issuing audit, monitoring, procurement, enforcement, or financial approval conclusions rather than assembling evidence and routing to the proper authority.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing official record or audit trail
Request that implies adjudication or enforcement authority
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Program-specific eligibility and appeal procedures vary by statute, agency, NOFO, and award terms.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require program statute, NOFO, agency policy, Notice of Award, and tenant authority map before producing administrative recommendations; escalate legal or adjudicative interpretation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public-records, FOIA, privacy, and retention obligations vary by jurisdiction and record custody.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require jurisdiction, custody, retention schedule, protected information classification, and public-records owner before any disclosure, deletion, publication, or withholding action.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Agency grants systems and recipient systems may use different source-of-truth rules for the same grant lifecycle status.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Preserve each record state, log source timestamps, and escalate to the official system owner when status conflicts affect action.
